input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
suno-meta: working Suno audio → lyrics+style extractor (FAI-0002)

Ship the tool formerly known as suno-extract-meta, renamed to suno-meta
for brevity. Three successful real-world runs on 2026-04-09:
- Vocal rock (Jacob Collier / Yellow cover) — lyrics + style extracted
- Instrumental bossa jazz BGM — validated BGM-mode extraction
- Tala's own "Unbroken Roots" cinematic forge track — PROVED Suno's
  upload-audio does real audio-to-lyric transcription, not just known-
  track matching (my earlier hypothesis was wrong)

The implementation went through iterative correction during the manual
integration test. Key fixes from the original spec:

1. AUTHORIZATION HEADER — the original task doc's HAR reverse-engineering
   missed the Authorization: Bearer <JWT> header entirely, claiming Suno
   used only a custom browser-token. Wrong. Suno uses Authorization +
   browser-token + device-id together. Added SUNO_AUTHORIZATION env var
   alongside SUNO_BROWSER_TOKEN and SUNO_DEVICE_ID, normalized the
   "Bearer " prefix (user can store with or without).

2. BROWSER FINGERPRINT HEADERS — Suno's backend validates Client Hints
   and fetch-metadata headers that modern Chrome sends. Added the full
   set: sec-ch-ua, sec-ch-ua-mobile, sec-ch-ua-platform, sec-fetch-dest,
   sec-fetch-mode, sec-fetch-site, priority, accept-language, user-agent
   (pinned to Chrome 147). Without these, Suno returns status=error
   during polling — a bot-detection signal.

3. PACING — the HAR-observed browser session had 400-4000ms intervals
   between steps and 4s polling intervals. Our original zero-delay
   implementation was 4x faster than a real browser. Added a PACING
   constants object with values ≥1.5x the HAR timings. Critical: 5000ms
   wait after step 4 (polling complete) before step 5 (initialize-clip)
   because Suno's image generation pipeline races with set_metadata's
   moderation check. Without the wait, step 6 returns image_moderation_
   error every time. Total wall-clock: ~40-50s per extraction (vs ~25s
   for a real browser session — we're deliberately slower).

4. IMAGE MODERATION RETRY — added a specific retry-once-after-5s for
   the image_moderation_error transient case. Detects the error by its
   error_type field in the step 6 response (Suno returns 200 with an
   embedded error_type, not an HTTP error).

5. CHECK-TOKEN ENDPOINT — original used GET on /api/uploads/audio/
   which is POST-only (returns 405 regardless of auth). Switched to
   GET /api/billing/info/ per user suggestion — cheap, authenticated,
   zero side effects, clearly distinguishes 200 (valid) from 401
   (expired).

6. STEP 5 CLIP_ID RESILIENCE — initialize-clip response may omit
   image_url or return it empty while the image is still generating
   server-side. Added fallback to construct the URL from the stable
   pattern https://cdn2.suno.ai/image_{clip_id}.jpeg. Also added
   defensive fallbacks for clip_id at nested paths (data.clip?.id,
   data.clips[0]?.id) so future response-shape drift logs the actual
   key structure instead of a generic "missing id" error.

7. POLLING PROGRESSION LOGGING — step 4 now logs the full status
   progression to stderr (e.g., "[poll] 5.7s status=passed_audio_
   processing"). Debuggability win — when polling fails, the last-
   status error message now includes the full progression.

8. ERROR MESSAGES carry more context — polling failures include the
   full status progression, not just the last status. set_metadata
   error_type responses are parsed and surfaced with both error_type
   and moderation_error_message.

Files:
- src/libs/suno-api/index.ts — 7-step flow, header builder, types,
  error class, sleep helper, pacing constants. Extracted from the
  CLI tool per A1 threshold during Ryan's initial implementation.
- src/tools/suno-meta.ts — thin CLI wrapper: arg parsing, toolDef
  export, checkToken, setup-help, pretty output formatting.
- src/tools/__tests__/suno-meta.test.ts — 29 unit tests, 133 expect
  calls. Covers happy path, every error code, trailing-slash
  asymmetry, secret-hygiene grep assertions.

Rename from suno-extract-meta → suno-meta per user preference for
brevity. Git-tracked rename, history preserved.

What's out of scope for this commit (queued as follow-ups):
- FAI-0003: Music card pipeline integration. Goal is to build a corpus
  of Suno-extracted data for both BGM and non-BGM tracks, then study
  the drift patterns to inform Tala's craft rules and reference files.
- FAI-0004: Token-refresh handshake. The Authorization JWT expires
  periodically (observed during testing — stale auth caused mid-flow
  failures). Currently user refreshes manually by updating .env; a
  future enhancement could auto-detect 401 and prompt for refresh.
- Minor cleanups from McCall's code review (duplicate afterEach,
  check-token lib extraction, polling timeout direct test).

Spec-gap learnings captured in this commit (for future HAR reverse-
engineering work):
- Always enumerate EVERY header on protected requests. Standard
  headers like Authorization get skipped because they "look normal."
- Browser fingerprint headers (sec-ch-*, sec-fetch-*) matter for
  sites that do bot detection. Assume they're needed until proven
  otherwise.
- HAR timing analysis is mandatory for rate-sensitive APIs. Our
  original spec had no timing consideration and hit transient
  failures that were pacing-related.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push origin main 2>&1 | tail -10

---
name: suno-meta tool (FAI-0002, shipped 2026-04-09)
description: Working Suno upload-audio extractor — call with an mp3, get lyrics + style prose back. Integration with /create-music-card is FAI-0003 (next).
type: project
---

# suno-meta tool — shipped 2026-04-09

## What it does
Uploads an mp3 to Suno's upload-audio endpoint and extracts Suno's own Lyrics + Style prose interpretation of the audio. Proves Suno does real audio-to-lyric transcription (not just known-track database matching).

## Run it
```
bun run tool suno-meta <path.mp3> [--json]
bun run tool suno-meta --check-token
bun run tool suno-meta --setup-help
```

## Files
- `src/tools/suno-meta.ts` — CLI wrapper
- `src/libs/suno-api/index.ts` — 7-step flow, headers, pacing, types
- `src/tools/__tests__/suno-meta.test.ts` — 29 unit tests

## Env vars (in `.env`, gitignored)
- `SUNO_AUTHORIZATION` — Bearer JWT from Chrome DevTools (with or without "Bearer " prefix)
- `SUNO_BROWSER_TOKEN` — Suno's secondary JSON token (wrapped in double quotes — it's a JSON object)
- `SUNO_DEVICE_ID` — UUID device identifier

## Timing
~40–50 seconds per extraction. Pacing is deliberate (1.5–3× the HAR-observed browser intervals) to avoid rate limiting. Do not reduce without evidence.

## Critical known gotchas
1. **Trailing slash asymmetry:** `set_metadata/` has trailing slash, `set_audio_description` does NOT. Two distinct URL constants.
2. **Authorization header is mandatory** — early spec missed this, assuming only browser-token was needed. Wrong.
3. **Browser fingerprint headers are mandatory** — sec-ch-ua, sec-fetch-*, user-agent, priority. Without them Suno returns status=error during polling.
4. **5-second wait after polling complete, before initialize-clip** — otherwise step 6 hits image_moderation_error because the clip image hasn't been generated server-side yet.
5. **Auth tokens expire** — observed mid-session during testing. User refreshes manually by updating `.env`.

## Proven working on
- Vocal rock (Jacob Collier / Yellow cover) — 2.2MB, 96s, extracted full lyrics
- Instrumental bossa jazz BGM (japan-cafe-light-jazz.mp3) — 7MB, 300s, extracted structural brackets
- Tala cinematic forge track (Unbroken Roots) — 4.5MB, 198s, real audio-to-lyric transcription confirmed

## Integration roadmap
- **FAI-0003 (next):** Add suno-meta as a step in `/create-music-card`. When building a music card, call suno-meta after the librosa analysis, save the readback as new frontmatter fields: `suno_style_prose`, `suno_lyrics`, `suno_display_tags`, `suno_has_vocal`, `suno_duration_seconds`. Both BGM and non-BGM tracks. This is the core ASK: build a corpus for studying Suno's interpretation layer.
- **NOT doing:** `/create-track` integration. User explicitly rejected this.
- **Goal of the corpus:** study patterns in Suno's interpretations, refine Tala's reference files and craft rules based on empirical drift data (e.g., "bodhran reads as bass drum", "C Phrygian becomes D minor", "Celtic neofolk drifts to cinematic folk rock").

## Related context
- Original task doc: `vault/notes/tasks/2026-04-09-190554-FAI-0002-suno-extract-meta.md` (filename still has old tool name, content updated)
- First drift finding: Tala's "Unbroken Roots" (C Phrygian, 68 BPM, basso profundo, bodhran) was heard by Suno as (D minor, 85 BPM, baritone, bass drum + taiko). Documented in commit ae2a570 as proof-of-concept for the empirical feedback loop.
- ASL (autonomous self-learning) was archived earlier same session. This tool is the replacement concept: grounded-in-Suno-data craft refinement instead of agent self-reflection.

## Ready to /clear or /compact

Your next command should be one of:
- **`/clear`** — full context wipe, fresh start for FAI-0003
- **`/compact`** — compress the current conversation, preserve key decisions in the compressed summary

I recommend **`/clear`** since FAI-0003 is a new task with a clean scope, and the memory file I just wrote contains everything you need to resume. The compressed form of this conversation would carry a lot of dead weight (ASL archive, /create-track template lock-in, 3 commits worth of work) that's already committed to git and doesn't need to live in context.

When you come back, start with `/morning` — the briefing will pull in the updated memory, you'll see suno-meta as an active project, and we can dispatch Holmes → McCall → Ryan for FAI-0003 with a clean slate.

**See you next session. Good work today.**

input.command: bun run tool reflect --agent freddie --task "Major session: archived ASL subsystem, locked Phase 4 template, built suno-meta tool end-to-end, shipped Silk and Breeze track" --workflow bye --outcome success --trace "Morning: reviewed 6 Tala soul proposals, rejected all 6 (4 were reference-derived rules, wrong class for soul). Briefed reference-drift filter, pivoted to knowledge classifier after McCall's discovery falsified cosine-threshold hypothesis (0.40 known-negative > 0.31 known-positive). User recognized pattern — ASL pipeline bloat, each layer fixing previous layer's mistakes. Decision: archive entire ASL subsystem. content.text: Reflect parse failure again (3rd time today — known bug, separate from this work). Logging the session's load-bearing learnings directly to brain.db as a workaround:

input.command: bun run tool brain --decide --agent freddie --decision "HAR reverse-engineering playbook: when spec'ing an API tool from network capture, enumerate EVERY header on protected requests including the standard ones (Authorization, User-Agent, Origin, Referer, sec-ch-*, sec-fetch-*). Standard-looking headers get skipped because they don't look custom, and that's how entire auth mechanisms get missed." --context "Session 2026-04-09 suno-meta build. Original HAR explorer pass said 'No Authorization header, Suno uses browser-token.' Wrong — Suno uses BOTH. Auth header got missed in two explorer passes because it looked standard. Browser fingerprint headers (sec-ch-ua, sec-fetch-*) were also missed and caused status=error polling failures when the tool was tested live." 2>&1 | tail -3

The 70-90 range in references measures the FELT tempo (half-time perception due to bossa clave), not the notated tempo. Real bossa nova is typically 120-140 BPM. The BPM gap is a reference file data-quality issue, not a Tala judgment issue. Fix target: vault/studio/references/suno/global-sounds.md and recipes-jazz.md bossa nova entries." 2>&1 | tail -3

## ✅ Saved, reflected, consolidated

| Phase | Status |
|---|---|
| **Save (raw context)** | ✅ auto-captured by hooks (no manual save needed — save-raw tool is deprecated) |
| **Reflect** | ⚠️ parse failure (3x today — known bug in reflect.ts). Worked around via manual decision logging. |
| **Decisions logged** | ✅ 4 new decisions to brain.db (#31–34) |
| **Consolidate** | ✅ all agent inboxes empty (expected — reflect bug blocked inbox writes today) |
| **Brain sync** | ✅ everything up to date |
| **Daily note** | ✅ wrap entry logged at 8:56 PM |
| **Memory system** | ✅ suno-meta project file + MEMORY.md index updated earlier |

### Key learnings captured as brain.db decisions

- **#31 (freddie)** — HAR reverse-engineering playbook: enumerate EVERY header including standard ones (Authorization, User-Agent, sec-ch-*, sec-fetch-*)
- **#32 (tala)** — Unbroken Roots drift patterns: bodhran→bass drum, C Phrygian→D minor, basso profundo→baritone, Celtic neofolk→cinematic folk rock
- **#33 (tala)** — Bossa nova BPM reference correction: refs say 70-90, real recordings sit at 120-140 (half-time perception vs notated tempo). Fix target: `references/suno/global-sounds.md` + `recipes-jazz.md`
- **#34 (freddie)** — ASL→suno-meta transition rationale: empirical audio feedback beats agent self-reflection

### One follow-up item surfaced

**reflect.ts parse failure** — happened 3 times today (on each of: create-track track build, bye skill, session wrap). The tool calls Gemini successfully but can't parse the response. Not blocking today, but it means **no inbox items are being written automatically**, which means consolidation has nothing to work on. Worth a look next time someone touches `src/tools/reflect.ts`.

---

## Ready to `/clear`

Everything persisted:
- ✅ 3 commits on origin/main (ASL archive, Phase 4 template, suno-meta)
- ✅ 8 brain.db decisions logged today (#27–34)
- ✅ Memory file at `~/.claude-personal/projects/d--PROJECTS-freddie-ai/memory/project_suno_meta.md`
- ✅ MEMORY.md index updated with suno-meta as active project
- ✅ Daily note has full session log
- ✅ FAI-0001 retro-audit task queued in vault/notes/tasks/

When you come back: run `/morning`, you'll see the suno-meta entry in active projects, then say "let's do FAI-0003" and we dispatch Holmes → McCall → Ryan for the `/create-music-card` integration.

Great session. Type `/clear` when ready.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: d:\PROJECTS\freddie-ai\.claude\skills\bye

End the current session cleanly.

****

## What This Does

`/bye` is the session closer — the counterpart to `/morning`. Triggers reflection, syncs brain.db, and logs to the daily note.

Consolidation and distillation now run asynchronously in the ASL daemon (ASL-0007) on file-change events. `/bye` stays fast (~5 seconds). If the daemon is down, run `bun run tool asl-sync` for a one-shot manual catch-up.

Raw context capture is handled automatically by the Stop hook (`capture-raw.ts`). No manual capture needed.

For formal dev sessions with task management, use `/dev-end` instead.

## Execution Model

**Freddie handles everything inline.** No sub-agents.

## Workflow

### Phase 1 — Reflect

Identify session metadata:
- **Agents involved** — freddie, mccall, wick, tala, etc.
- **Skill/workflow** — what was the session about
- **Summary** — one-line summary (use  if provided)
- **Outcome** — success, partial, or failed

```bash
bun run tool reflect --agent freddie --task "{summary}" --workflow {skill} --outcome {success/partial/failed} --trace "{brief trace}"
```

### Phase 2 — Sync brain.db

```bash
bun run tool brain --check
```

### Phase 3 — Daily Log

Log the session to today's daily note:

```bash
bun run tool obsidian sections add-log --content "Session: {summary}. Agents: {list}."
```

### Phase 4 — Sign Off

Print:
```
Session saved.
  Reflect: {learnings} learnings, {decisions} decisions
  Daily: logged
  ASL daemon: {running (PID: N) | stopped — run `npm run asl:start` to enable background learning}

See you next time.
```

<!-- Note: The ASL daemon status shown in Phase 4 is informational only. /bye does NOT
     gate on daemon liveness — if the daemon is down, reflect still ran and the memory
     file is written. The canonical daemon warning surface is startup.ts (SessionStart).
     If checking the PID file here adds complexity, skip it — the startup probe covers it. -->

---

## Why /bye is fast

Consolidation and distillation used to run inline here. They now run in the ASL
daemon (ASL-0007) on file-change events. If the daemon is down, run
`bun run tool asl-sync` to catch up manually. The daemon status is surfaced
at the start of every session by `.claude/hooks/startup.ts`.

Session close — most phases already ran in the previous turn. Just the sign-off.

**Heads up:** this `/bye` skill doc still references ASL (archived today). I'm flagging it, not fixing it — new file changes after commit/push would muddy the close. Queue it as a quick cleanup next session.

---

```

  Commits:        3 pushed to origin/main (5b59473, 4576ab7, ae2a570)
  Decisions:      8 logged to brain.db (#27–34)
  Reflect:        parse failure 3x (known bug, worked around)
  Brain.db:       up to date
  Daily:          logged (2026-04-09)
  Memory:         suno-meta project file saved
  ASL daemon:     archived (no longer exists, this skill doc is stale)

Session stats:
  ├── ASL subsystem archived (48 files, −515 lines)
  ├── Phase 4 review template locked in (5 files, +221 lines)
  ├── suno-meta tool shipped end-to-end (3 files, +179 lines)
  ├── Silk and Breeze track approved first-try in Suno
  ├── Unbroken Roots drift analysis surfaced 5 craft insights
  └── FAI-0003 queued for next session

Follow-ups queued:
  • FAI-0001  soul file retro-audit (low priority)
  • FAI-0003  /create-music-card + suno-meta integration (next)
  • FAI-0004  Suno token refresh handshake (future)
  • reflect.ts parse failure bug
  • /bye skill doc ASL cleanup

See you next time, sir.
```
